---
title: "Compartmental Epidemic Models: From SIR to Spatial Stochastic Dynamics"
subtitle: "Track A — Biostatistics / Track E Overlap"
date: "2025-01-01"
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
number-sections: true
---

# Compartmental Epidemic Models: From SIR to Spatial Stochastic Dynamics

**Track**: Track A — Biostatistics / Track E Overlap

The mathematical theory of infectious disease transmission. Covers deterministic and stochastic compartmental models, R₀ theory, spatial spread, network epidemiology, and parameter estimation from surveillance data.

## Table of Contents

1. The SIR Model and Its Variants
2. The Basic Reproduction Number R₀
3. Endemic Equilibria and Stability
4. Stochastic Epidemic Models
5. Age-Structured and Multi-Group Models
6. Spatial Epidemic Models
7. Parameter Estimation and Model Fitting
8. Optimal Control of Epidemics

---

## Chapter 1: The SIR Model and Its Variants

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 2: The Basic Reproduction Number R₀

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 3: Endemic Equilibria and Stability

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 4: Stochastic Epidemic Models

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 5: Age-Structured and Multi-Group Models

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 6: Spatial Epidemic Models

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 7: Parameter Estimation and Model Fitting

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 8: Optimal Control of Epidemics

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*
